title: asplenium adiantum-nigrum var. incisum
intro: Asplenium adiantum-nigrum var. incisum, also known as the black spleenwort or black maiden's hair fern, is a variety of fern that belongs to the family Aspleniaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Asplenium adiantum-nigrum var. incisum include reducing swelling and pain, as well as protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
